query: What edge cases does this vault demonstrate?
answer:
Let me look at the vault contents to answer this.Let me read the key files to understand the edge cases demonstrated.Now let me look at a few more specific pages that deal with edge cases.Let me look at a few more targeted pages to get the full picture of edge cases.Now I have a thorough view of the vault. Here's a summary of the edge cases it demonstrates:

---

## Edge Cases Demonstrated by This Vault

### 1. git-worktree auto-memory leaks to parent repo
`concepts/claude-code-auto-memory-isolation.md` — Claude Code follows git-worktree linkage and keys auto-memory to the *parent* repo path, not the worktree path. A harness using `git worktree add` for isolation does NOT get auto-memory isolation. Fix: `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY=1` (which also preserves OAuth; `--bare` does not).

### 2. Wikilink scanner including frontmatter and code-spans
`log.md [2026-05-09]` — The linter scanned frontmatter fields and backtick prose examples for wikilinks, producing 7 false-positive broken-link findings. Link-shaped tokens inside YAML or inline code were not excluded.

### 3. Lint code_drift slug normalization — facet sub-pages treated as packages
`sources/2026-05-lattice-wiki-core-lint-code-drift-slug-normalization.md` — Sub-pages (`api.md`, `context.md`, `patterns.md`, `work.md`) inherited `category: package` and leaked into code_drift comparisons, producing 47 false-positive `orphaned_in_vault` entries. Fix: structural invariant `Path(k).parent.name == Path(k).name` to identify only overview pages.

### 4. `lint --json` emitting `null` vs `{"skipped": true}`
`sources/2026-05-lattice-wiki-core-three-wiki-bug-fixes.md` — Five drift fields initialized to `None`; `json.dumps(default=list)` does NOT coerce explicit `None` to `[]`. Callers couldn't distinguish "check skipped (no repo_path)" from "check ran clean." Fixed with a `_SKIPPED` sentinel dict.

### 5. `FILE_MAP_SECTION_RE` parser bugs — nested bullets and missing-name headers
`log.md [2026-05-10]` — Two bugs: (a) the section-entry parser didn't track bullet indentation, so nested bullets generated 109 false-positive file-map drift entries; (b) `## File map` without a package-name prefix caused the first bullet to be captured as `pkg_name`.

### 6. `CLAUDE.md.template` unconditionally claiming `AGENTS.md` exists
Bug fix source — The template always referenced a parallel `AGENTS.md`, but `init_wiki` only writes it when `--tool` is `codex|cursor|antigravity|opencode|gemini-cli|all`, not the default `claude-code` init.

### 7. Index taxonomy leaking `work.md` sub-pages into package count
Bug fix source — `packages/*/work.md` files weren't in `SUBPAGE_STEMS`, so they were counted in the `## Package` section (showing 24 instead of 12). Also, the `## More` section went invisible when a category had 0 pages.

### 8. `graph_analyzer.py` workspace-prefix blindness (post-ADR-0015)
`log.md [2026-05-10]` — After ADR-0015 rewrote wikilinks to workspace-root-relative form (`[[wiki/...]]`), the graph analyzer didn't recognize the new prefix and reported 47 false-positive orphans because it still expected bare container-relative paths.

### 9. `index.md` outbound links not validated by linter
`log.md [2026-05-10]` — The linter excluded `index.md` from the page-set entirely, so broken outbound links in `index.md` (e.g., `[[wiki/architecture/index]]` before that stub was created) were invisible to lint.

### 10. State gate / dirty tree blocking `last_sync_commit` bumps
`log.md` (recurring) — Every release scan was blocked from bumping `last_sync_commit` because the working tree was dirty (`.lattice.yaml`, `uv.lock`, `.pyc` files). This was a known recurring pattern that accumulated sync drift across 6+ consecutive release scans.

### 11. Historical wikilinks to archived work items
`log.md [2026-05-12]` — When 17 work items were archived (moved to `work/archived/`), existing wikilinks in ADR pages and patterns pages pointed to the old paths. The linter flagged 42 broken links, but the right remediation was ambiguous: rewrite for lint cleanness vs. preserve for historical fidelity.

### 12. pcvelz/superpowers fork: worktree detection regression
`concepts/superpowers-fork-vs-upstream.md` — The fork replaced git's robust `git rev-parse --git-dir` vs `--git-common-dir` check with a directory-name heuristic (`.worktrees/` naming). This misidentifies worktree state in any repo not following that naming convention.

### 13. `TaskGet` not returning `metadata` parameter — brittle JSON fence
Same concept page — The fork embeds metadata as a ` ```json:metadata` fence inside the task description because `TaskGet` doesn't return the `metadata` parameter. Any agent rewriting the description without preserving the fence silently loses structured state.

### 14. Skill resolution: bare name at plan time vs. fully-qualified at dispatch time
`concepts/lattice-skill-resolution-order.md` — Plans record bare skill names; dispatch resolves to fully-qualified at invocation time. If resolved names were frozen at plan time, user-level skill overrides added later would be bypassed silently.

### 15. Architecture/ADR index staleness — counted 15 ADRs when 16 existed
`log.md [2026-05-11]` — `architecture/index.md` hardcoded "15 ADRs" and was not regenerated when ADR-0016 was added. A stale count that linters repeatedly flagged as a semantic finding.

---

In summary, the vault is a live record of **linter tooling false-positives** (slug normalization, nested bullets, frontmatter scanner, graph prefix blindness, index exclusion), **isolation traps** (git-worktree auto-memory, OAuth vs. `--bare`), **JSON API contract bugs** (null vs. skipped, missing metadata in TaskGet), and **structural conventions that require invariants to enforce correctly** (overview-page filter, workspace-relative wikilinks, single-writer DB, explicit-not-magic update lifecycle).

<DONE>
